text: In C File Handling, what does the mode "a+" signify when opening a file?
options: Open for reading only; file must exist.
Open for writing only; overwrites the file if it exists.
Open for reading and appending; creates the file if it does not exist.
Open for reading and writing; overwrites the file.

text: In command line arguments `int main(int argc, char *argv[])`, what does `argv[0]` typically hold?
options: The first argument passed by the user.

options: Only static data members of the class.

options: To prevent the instantiation of the base class.

options: A friend function is a member of the class it is declared in.

options: Searches for the string ".c" inside all files in the current directory.

Finds all files ending with ".c" in the current directory and its subdirectories.

options: It is impossible to implement a Stack using Queues.

options: Two instructions attempt to use the same hardware resource simultaneously.

text: A 'System Call' in an operating system fundamentally generates a:

options: Threads do not use CPU registers.
Threads of the same process share the same virtual memory map (Page Tables), so it does not need to be flushed.
Threads execute sequentially and do not require the OS kernel.
Threads do not have their own individual stacks.

options: Containing at least one virtual function.
Containing at least one pure virtual function.

text: In a C++ `try-catch` block sequence, what happens if a base class `catch` block is placed before a derived class `catch` block?
options: The program will execute perfectly based on the thrown exception.
The compiler generates an error about invalid block ordering.

The derived class catch block will automatically override the base class block.

text: What is 'Template Specialization' in C++?
options: Defining a specific implementation of a template for a particular data type.
Restricting a template to only accept integer values.
Creating a template that works automatically for multiple inherited classes.
Using macros instead of inline functions.

text: Which of the following memory segments is NOT shared among multiple threads of the same process?

options: To load the OS kernel into memory during boot.
To act as a buffer for data traveling to slow peripheral devices like printers.

To manage CPU time slices among multiple users.

options: Any attribute that can uniquely identify a row.
A minimal superkey where no proper subset is also a superkey.

text: Which relational database constraint ensures that a value in one table must mathematically exist in another table?

options: B+ Trees have fewer levels than B-Trees.
B+ Trees store actual data records or data pointers ONLY at the leaf nodes.

text: In Software Design, which type of Cohesion is considered the highest quality and most desirable?

options: Testing performed internally by the development team.
Testing performed by an automated testing framework.
Testing performed by actual end-users in their own real-world environment.

text: The 'Naive Bayes' classification algorithm is considered 'Naive' primarily because it assumes:
options: That the dataset contains no outliers.
That all input features are conditionally independent of each other.

options: They retain their value between multiple function calls.
They are initialized to zero by default if not explicitly initialized.
They can be accessed from any other function in the same program.

text: Which of the following statements are TRUE regarding Constructors in C++?
options: They cannot have a return type, not even void.
They can be overloaded within a class.
They can be declared as virtual.

text: In C++ inheritance, if a base class is inherited 'protectedly', what happens to its members in the derived class?
options: Public members of the base class become protected in the derived class.
Protected members of the base class become protected in the derived class.
Private members of the base class become protected in the derived class.
Public members of the base class remain public in the derived class.
